name: Three-Column Browser
description: Three-column browse layout with navigation tree, item list, and detail preview. Classic mail/file-browser pattern. Used for email clients, file managers, documentation browsers, and any master-detail-detail interface.

tree.item_content: Folder/category icon + label. Supports nested tree with expand/collapse chevrons. Collapsed nav: icon only.

nav.note: Left navigation tree. Holds folder/category structure. Selecting an item populates the list column. Collapses to icon rail on tablet.

header.content: Selected folder name + item count + sort control

items.item_content: Item title (bold) + subtitle/preview (truncated, muted) + metadata (date, badge). Selected item has active background.

list.note: Middle item list. Shows items for the selected nav folder. Selecting an item populates the detail column. Items highlight on selection.

header.content: Item title + action buttons (reply, archive, delete, etc.)

body.note: Full content of the selected item.

empty_state.content: Placeholder illustration + 'Select an item to view' message
empty_state.note: Shown when no item is selected in the list.

detail.note: Right detail preview. Shows full content of the selected list item. Scrolls independently.

responsive.desktop: Three columns visible simultaneously, all scroll independently
responsive.tablet: Nav collapses to icon rail (56px), list and detail remain visible
responsive.mobile: Single column with back navigation. Nav as overlay drawer, list as primary view, detail as pushed view with back button

guidance.nav_purpose: Holds tree/folder structure for top-level navigation categories
guidance.list_purpose: Shows items belonging to the selected nav folder, acts as a scannable index
guidance.detail_purpose: Shows full content of the selected list item with actions
guidance.selection_flow: Nav folder -> List populates -> List item -> Detail populates
guidance.independent_scroll: All three columns scroll independently to maintain context across panels
guidance.list_highlight: Selected list item should have a visually distinct active/selected state
guidance.empty_detail: When no list item is selected, detail shows a centered empty-state placeholder
guidance.keyboard_nav: Arrow keys navigate list items, Enter opens in detail, Escape goes back to list on mobile
guidance.mobile_pattern: On mobile, columns become stacked views with back-navigation between them
guidance.shell_spacing: The shell owns panel sizing and inter-column rhythm. Route content should fill the declared columns rather than wrapping itself in additional max-width containers.

code.imports: Target-specific imports vary. Translate the internal_layout contract into your framework's left tree nav, middle item list, right detail panel, and shared browser header.
code.example: ThreeColumnBrowserShell(root) -> Header(search, sort, user actions) + TreeNav(folder hierarchy) + ItemList(selected collection) + DetailPane(selected document or API entry)
